item.title: Self-Employment Promotion as Active Labor Market Policy: Design and Trade-offs in Rigid Labor Markets
item.description: This paper studies how self-employment promotion can be designed as active labor-market policy in rigid labor markets.

item.title: Early-Career Job Instability and Life-Cycle Income Dynamics
Income Dynamics of Couples: Correlated Risks and Heterogeneous Within-Household Insurance
Spatial Fiscal Policy: Stimulus Payments in a Consumption Network
The Value of Public Insurance Against Idiosyncratic Income Risk: A Variance-Adjustment Statistic
Consumption Dynamics and Welfare Under Non-Gaussian Earnings Risk
item.description: This paper studies how earnings risk beyond a Gaussian benchmark changes household consumption dynamics and welfare.

item.title: University financing: sustainability, efficiency and redistribution
item.description: Book chapter on university financing and policy design.

item.title: La financiacion de las universidades: sostenibilidad, eficiencia y redistribucion
item.description: Spanish-language book chapter on university financing and policy design.

item.title: Does Self-Employment Pay? The Role of Unemployment and Earnings Risk
item.description: This paper evaluates self-employment returns while accounting for unemployment and earnings-risk trade-offs.

item.title: The Nonlinear Consumption Response to Income Risk: The Role of Tail Changes and Durables
item.description: Revise and resubmit, Review of Economic Dynamics.

item.title: Skewed Idiosyncratic Income Risk over the Business Cycle: Sources and Insurance
item.description: This paper measures asymmetric income risk over the business cycle and studies the insurance mechanisms that mitigate it.

item.title: Urban Welfare: Tourism in Barcelona
item.description: This project studies how tourism and local price changes affect urban welfare in Barcelona.

item.title: Income Contingent University Loans: Policy Design and an Application to Spain
item.description: This paper studies the design of income-contingent university loans and their distributional consequences.
